claim_map.claim_text: The input context is constrained to metadata-only use and does not authorize authoring, claim insertion, chapter updates, docs/book integration, or publication.

Any later use must frame the evidence narrowly: OpenClaw documentation names Hermes in migration/setup tooling contexts only, without stating that Hermes is a runtime dependency or general operating environment absent additional sources.
No publication approval, chapter update, claim insertion, or docs/book update is available from the provided context.

forbidden_claims_checked.forbidden_claim: Hermes is a runtime dependency of OpenClaw.
Hermes is the general operating environment for OpenClaw.
OpenClaw requires Hermes for web or phone access.
Migration/setup/import tooling adjacency proves architectural dependency.
The canary is publishable or chapter-ready.
The canary can be inserted as a claim without later gates.

findings.finding: Authoring is not allowed because author_allowed, eligible_for_authoring, eligible_for_claim_insertion, eligible_for_publication, chapter_update_allowed, and publication_approved are all false.

The packet preserves mandatory caveats and negative boundaries.
The disposition avoids unsupported market, dependency, access, operating-environment, and generalization claims.

publish_packets.proposed_markdown_delta: No docs/book change proposed in Run 43; packet is blocked or reports-only.

publication_readiness.reason: No docs/book update, chapter prose, publication approval, or guarded publication is permitted from advisory-only metadata. The packet is reports-only.

findings.finding: The input context explicitly forbids authoring, publication approval, claim insertion, chapter updates, and docs/book integration. Publication must remain blocked.
There is a known overpromotion risk: tooling-adjacency metadata could be incorrectly converted into runtime dependency or operating-environment language if the mandatory caveat is stripped.
Evidence is narrow, singleton-derived, and caveat-only; it is insufficient for broader claims or chapter-ready prose.
No privacy-invasive content or raw capture reproduction is required or included in this packet.

required_caveats: Frame narrowly: OpenClaw documentation names Hermes in migration/setup tooling contexts; do not state that Hermes is a runtime dependency or general operating environment without additional sources.
Do not promote tooling adjacency into architectural dependency, runtime dependency, web access dependency, phone access dependency, or operating-environment claims.
Do not use the material as chapter prose, publishable wording, or claim insertion before later gates supply adequate sources and approvals.
publish_packets.source_quality_summary: Source quality is narrow and constrained. The input is an advisory-only metadata/control object, not source material for prose. It preserves provenance, caveats, blockers, and negative boundaries, but it is singleton-derived and explicitly ineligible for authoring, publication, chapter updates, or claim insertion. It is suitable only for safe reports-only tracking or caveat-preserving planning metadata.
publish_packets.summary: The supplied context is advisory-only, metadata-only, ineligible for authoring, ineligible for claim insertion, and ineligible for publication. No chapter prose, docs/book update, or publication deployment is permitted. The only safe disposition is a reports-only packet preserving the caveat that OpenClaw documentation names Hermes only in migration/setup tooling contexts and does not support runtime dependency, operating-environment, or access-requirement claims.
publish_packets.title: Run 43 safe reports-only packet for constrained OpenClaw/Hermes metadata
